posts.author: ChrisSweeney
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 12 Winners, 5 Perfect, 580 Points
|
Tied for 276th

Burns vs. Malott
Mike Malott, TKO, R2
Malott -600???. Insane even for an ultra washed 40 year-old 0-4 Burns in Canada. Burns' losses have aged well and this is a step-down but does it matter when he’s losing every round in all of them? And you can literally see his decline. Physically and mentally. He’s been through a lot of damage and weightcuts, He looks delayed and slow on the feet, He doesn’t have much power, He cannot take a shot, And he just doesn’t fight like that dog he was before. His skill on the ground is still there but he’s not gonna control Malott for 5 rounds. All of this makes me feel like I have to pick Malott. But he’s nothing special either, that's the problem. A 34 year-old Canadian prospect who’s actually scared to fight. He’s been horrified ever since he fumbled the ball to Magny. Since then it’s been Trevin Giles, Shaky Radtke performance, And he barely beat Kevin Holland with an illegal shot. As much as I want to fade Malott finally I doubt he fumbles this, He somewhat has power. He can clip 40 year-old Gilbert Burns early.

Phillips vs. Jourdain
Charles Jourdain, DEC
I think Jourdain has looked good at 135, but he’s a little overrated. Kyler is a good fighter who looks great in round one, but has extreme cardio issues. Unless Kyler has completely fixed himself I can see Jourdain taking over. Jourdain has fumbled plenty of times though and kyler is a real fighter and has the better comp on paper. Jourdain is probably the more technical striker, He has a grappling advantage, Cardio advantage, Hometown advantage. Jourdain just has to get past round one and take over with volume if Kyler fades, and don’t get wrestle ******. It’s been done before. Kyler is still very live as a dog, this is a tricky match-up. Unless Jourdain pulls something crazy out of his toolbag I doubt there’s a finish. 3x 29-28 Jourdain. Might switch.

Nallo vs. Herbert
Mandel Nallo, TKO, R2
I wanna take the underdog so bad. I like Jai. He's a good NPC fighter Good crafty striking, Good ground defense, Who has the experience with better competition. The issue is he fights to his competition level and he has a suspicious chin. Nallo isn’t a huge dropoff in the level of fighter, and he has KO power. He’s never seen a decision. Nallo had a good DWCS performance as the underdog he got a KO against a pretty durable opponent using leg kicks and just put him down simply with a straight right. It’s obvious Nallo knows how to finish a fight and Jai has a bad chin and is due for a KO. Jai could get crafty but I think Nallo finds the shot.

Jasudavicius vs. Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Good match-making for the division. Good bounce back fight for Jasmine. She got embarrassed by Fiorot and her iced out Labubu, but Fiorot’s a monster so I'll let it slide. Karine’s coming off competitive fight with Maycee Barber where she didn’t look bad her offensive grappling is there but her defense kinda let her down in the ground exchanges. Both these fighters are primarily grapplers. And I think Jasmine is the better of the two. Neither are much on their feet. Karine doesn’t do great off her back, and Jasmine is stronger and a better wrestler. She can definitely control Karine. I don’t think Karine’s finishing ability is there vs. high comp. Jasmine probably has the better chance to finish since she’s active on top. 3x 30-27 Jasudavicius.

Moisés vs. Young
Gauge Young, DEC
Should be a very competitive fight that could go either way, weird match-making though. Young has the edge on the feet and could hurt Moises, his pace and aggression should make a difference since Moises doesn’t bring much striking outside of leg kicks and his reactions aren’t great. Young’s wrestling is solid too, so it’s hard to see Moises getting him down easily and controlling him. Moises still has the BJJ, IQ, and experience, but he looked bad against Jared Gordon and has seemed a bit checked out lately, which makes him tough to trust. Gordon’s a good veteran though, it makes you wonder if Young is even good enough or just in a favorable matchup stylistically.

Buzukja vs. Barbosa
Márcio Barbosa, TKO, R1
Should be an easy fight for Barbosa. Funny enough Barbosa is another regional Gabriel Santos victim, how good is Gabriel Santos? Buzujka is a bit of a jobber and not really good at anything, 1-3 in the UFC against decent comp in his losses. He’s gonna fight the exact way Barbosa wants. Buzujka doesn’t have much power to catch Barbosa and he’s hittable. Barbosa is a good striker, very heavy hitter and is a round one or nothing fighter. He should be fun in 145. I don’t see this getting out the first round.

Valentin vs. Leblanc
Robert Valentin, SUB, R2
Regional MMA fight. Never would I think I’d pick Valentin to ever win a UFC fight. But here we are. Leblanc is just awful. Yes Valentin has looked awful in the UFC, but he fought a monster in Ateba, and a complete mismatch in FInney and Loder. Now he gets to fight a middle aged Canadian regional can. Valentin’s TDD is abysmal but he has grappling going for him at least. He should definitely have the advantage on the mat. Leblanc probably has the slight edge in striking but he’s still not good and Valentin can make it competitive. Not like I watched every Leblanc fight but in the one’s I did I saw that he goes into the clinch a lot. I think that’s better for Valentin than him. Maybe Leblanc catches him, but I think Valentin saves his job. He’s more experienced and better on the mat. I think he finds the sub. We'll see how it goes in Canada.

Boser vs. Saricam
Gokhan Saricam, DEC
Another regional MMA fight, this is crazy match-making, this could be the worst fight ever. Both dudes do absolutely nothing in the octagon; they just circle around the entire fight with no output. Both dudes can’t strike or grapple. It’s gonna be a slow low-level sparring match. But one’s dudes coming off a decent Bellator run and one is coming off a 2 and half near layoff. Boser sucks completely and has lost fights simply because his TDD is so bad. I expect a blob fest where Saricam wrestles Boser, probably does slightly more on the feet and wins an awful unanimous decision. 3x 29-28 Saricam.

Croden vs. Zheleznyakova
Melissa Croden, DEC
This is a decent WMMA fight. Both these girls come to fight, are mostly strikers, both get hit a lot. I’m lowk a part of the Croden army. I’ve picked her in her last two and I kinda just wanna keep it going. I like her activity. She’s a good striker, she has a base of wrestling at least, big and physical for the division, and she walks forward and throws down. Zheleznyakova is a bit of the same, without any mat skills. A good striker that throws hard. The tricky thing is Croden uses her size, and Zheleznyakova is the same height and build. It’ll still be competitive, neither woman wears damage well at all. If Croden wrestled a little more then she did I would have an easier time picking her. Croden via split decision. 50/50 fight. Might switch.

Aldrich vs. Horth
Jamey-Lyn Horth, DEC
WMMA fight. Both mediocre. Horth is coming off a quick and impressive KO. Aldrich is coming off a boring decision with a 13-11 fighter. Horth’s physicality and power, along with the fight being in Canada is enough for me to pick her here. Aldrich can’t hurt Horth and can’t wrestle her enough for it to be useful. Probably a really boring fight unless Horth somehow finds a second KO in a row but I doubt it. 3x 29-28 Horth.

Castañeda vs. Vologdin
John Castañeda, DEC
This is one of the bright-spots on this dreadful card. This will be a banger. Castaneda isn’t a world-beater, but he can compete in the UFC, is well rounded, and pushes a great pace. He has decent wins in Miles Johns and Gufarov, and performed well in his losses. Vologdin had an amazing fight on the contender series where I actually picked him, and while it was a great fight he was on the losing end in his first fight against real comp. Also Vologdin is way too small for Bantamweight, he’s shown great finishing ability but I don’t know if he’ll have it at 135 in the UFC. But he’s very tough, he has mad grit, he has decent striking with body work, crazy pressure with big shots. If he’s moving forward in this fight and he can defend the wrestling he has a real chance to win. If he loses he’s still 25 I’d like to see him at 125 with the experience of Castaneda and his power maximized. 3x 29-28 Castaneda. Might switch.

Siraj vs. Yannis
Jamie Siraj, SUB, R2
Yes this fight totally needed to be added of course. I don't think Siraj is amazing, he starts slow but the blueprint to beat Yannis is out there. Get him down, dudes clueless on the mat. Siraj’s offensive wrestling isn’t anything great, but he can find the back and get submissions, which is all he really needs here. Yannis has better stand-up than Siraj but I don’t think it will be there long enough for it to make a huge impact. Siraj is coming down from 145 so he’ll have a size advantage too. Siraj plays it smart in his debut in Canada and gets the submission.

posts.author: connorbrown16
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 12 Winners, 4 Perfect, 440 Points
|
Tied for 2886th

Jamie Siraj vs. John Yannis
Jamie Siraj via 2nd Round Submission
Yannis really isn’t UFC caliber if we’re going to be honest and he has a tendency to get caught in submissions if a fight goes long with enough grappling exchanges. That plays right into Siraj’s wheelhouse and I don’t see Yannis catching him clean. Once Siraj takes his back and locks in the RNC in the 2nd, that’ll be all she wrote.

John Castaneda vs. Mark Vologdin
John Castaneda via Decision
Again, I gotta see it first to believe it from any contract winner from last seasons Contenders Series. Not only that but Vologdin is really small and Castaneda will benefit from the weight change. Plus he’s on a two fight skid and can’t afford to drop a third so I’ll take the vet getting back in the win column with a decision victory.

JJ Aldrich vs. Jamey-Lyn Horth
Jamey-Lyn Horth via Split Decision
I really have little to nothing to say about this fight except they usually give JJ the easiest fights imaginable but this is tricky. Not only is Horth gritty but it’s a home fight for her and this is definitely going to the judges. This gives me the Greasy Creepys but I’ll take Horth to get it done in front of the hometown fans.

Melissa Croden vs. Darya Zheleznyakova
Darya Zheleznyakova via Split Decision
Man, I’m really starting to hate this card at this point. Let’s throw some monkey wrenches into this. I think Croden is terrible but again, this is in Canada. That being said, if she stinks up the joint enough all Darya needs is two judges to be somewhat paying attention to the fight and not scrolling through Instagram on their phones to win the fight. I have enough faith that the Canadian judges will do the right thing and not award their countrywomen because she’s the drizzling ****s. A double Greasy Banana Split for the Winnipeg fans but the Russian takes this one.

Tanner Boser vs. Gokhan Saricam
Tanner Boser via 1st Round TKO
I was going to **** on this fight and probably still should but what would be the fun in that? Saricam isn’t very good, don’t be fooled and he’s 35 getting his first baptism in the UFC. Boser is getting his 2nd chance but I liked what I heard from him earlier this week. He’s here to have fun and put on a show for the fans and I think Hulk Hokit lit a fire under some of these Heavyweights because he made huge leaps in a short amount of time. Boser will state into the fire and go to war with Saricam and it’ll be another chaotic battle but this ain’t going to the bell, or the stools for that matter. Bulldozer lives up to his name and crushes Saricam and sets the seed for the eventual Boser vs Hulk Hokit fight for the ownership of Canada.

Robert Valentin vs. Julien Leblanc
Robert Valentin via Decision
Robzilla is due for a win and Leblanc hasn’t paid his UFC dues yet. Valentin is also one to embrace a fire fight and will want to test Leblanc’s chin as soon as possible. I don’t see Leblanc withstanding an onslaught if Valentin turns up the pace on him but I can see Valentin letting him off the hook instead of pressing his luck and getting caught in something. Robzilla takes the decision win.

Dennis Buzukja vs. Marcio Barbosa
Marcio Barbosa via 1st Round TKO
This feels like they’re pushing Buzukja out the door and feeding him to a rabid wolverine in Barbosa. This will be over in minutes and in brutal fashion as Barbosa destroys Buzukja and maybe to the regional circuit.

Thiago Moises vs. Gauge Young
Gauge Young via Decision
I’m not necessarily “high” on Gauge but he is improving each outing and is still just 25. He’s showing more confidence on the feet and can hold his own fending off takedowns and any grappling his opponent trying to deploy. Moises doesn’t handle faster strikers all that well and this sets up for Gauge to take over as the fight wears on. Moises might have a few moments of his own but it won’t be enough as Gee Money makes it two in a row in the UFC.

Jasmine Jasudavicius vs. Karine Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius via Decision
I’ve never been sold on Karine and she’s a quitter when the going gets tough. Jas is never going to be a champion but at least she takes herself serious and I really can’t say the same for Karine half the time. She also is a notorious gasser as the fight wears on and Jas will just wrestle her to a decision.

Mandel Nallo vs. Jai Herbert
Mandel Nallo via 1st Round TKO
Now we get to the feel-good moment of the night. Look at this, just a couple of Unc’s (Can you classify all fighters 36 or older in any weight 155lbs or under because I just did) wanting to beat the ever loving **** out of each other in front of a bunch of Winnipegians (I just created a term for you Winnipeg, you’ll get use to it when you’re eventually apart of America) Anywell what were we talking about? Oh yea, never picking Jai Herbert to win a fight again. He’s too timid and not a threat anymore while Nallo will waste no time here. I expect him to push a fast pace, get Herbert to the ground, get his back and flatten him out, then rain hell on him til the ref waves it off. Dude has been waiting an eternity for this and it’ll be so awesome to see him celebrate this in front of his fellow countrymen.

Kyler Phillips vs. Charles Jourdain
Charles Jourdain via Decision
No one has used the Guillotine since medieval times like Charles Jourdain but Phillips will be smart to stay out of it in this matchup. I’m just not overly impressed with where Phillips is and thought he’d have a more developed arsenal at this point. Jourdain has some sharp hands and can keep Phillips at bay from a distance and take a comfortable decision win.

Gilbert Burns vs. Mike Malott
Mike Malott via 1st Round TKO
I’ll make this short and sweet. Malott isn’t anything special but Gil is beyond shot at this point. He’s just here to get one last UFC check. Malott finishes him in minutes. Gil, please retire already.

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 12 Winners, 6 Perfect, 630 Points
|
Tied for 90th

Burns vs. Malott
Mike Malott, TKO, R1
Sorry, Gilbert, but it's time. I don't think Mike Malott can really hang with the likes of JDM, Ian Garry, Michael Morales, etc., but Burns is legitimately done. Yes, he's only lost to very high-level competition, but he's nearly 40 and coming off of losses where he was KO'd, outwrestled, and KO'd again. Gilbert needs takedowns and control to win this, but Malott's got the wrestling skills to prevent that from happening. The best thing Gilbert can hope for is to keep the fight going long enough for Malott to gas like he did against Magny, but I'm not so sure that Gilbert himself has enough gas left in the tank to make that happen. Malott's got some pretty underrated hands, as well. Outside of a flash TKO, I don't see Gilbert winning this.

Phillips vs. Jourdain
Charles Jourdain, DEC
I hope I don't jinx this, but Charles feels like a lock in this spot. Kyler is not Jean Silva, so I find it very unlikely that he'll decapitate Jourdain in the first 7.5 minutes of the fight. While Jourdain has fumbled some winnable fights like Erosa, Andre Fili, etc., I think he's entering his prime now, and he's surpassed my expectations since moving down from 145 to 135. Phillips will likely gas and lose R2 and R3. I could see Jourdain potentially getting a late sub or KO, but Kyler's never been subbed and that's Jourdain's easiest path to a finish. I think very highly of Jourdain’s skillset and hope to see him do well. He also seems like a chill dude on social media. Sorry I cheered when Jean Silva missed weight and uppercutted you, bro.

Nallo vs. Herbert
Mandel Nallo, TKO, R1
Could be a sneaky underdog play here. Herbert's faced the much better slate of competition, and Nallo is making his UFC debut at nearly 37 years old. That's not a good recipe for success, but Herbert hasn't looked himself in the last few fights, and he's definitely the fighter with a lot more wear and tear here despite the two guys being close in age and close in number of fights. Nallo's apparently got a reputation around Tristar for being one of the most talented guys there, but gym rumors are gym rumors. Nallo also fades badly whenever fights leave R1, and Herbert is not an easy guy to put away. Still, I'll go with Rat Garbage.

Jasudavicius vs. Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Karine Silva's best path to victory here is the wrestling, but Jasmine's the better wrestler. That's as much thought as I've put into this.

Moisés vs. Young
Gauge Young, DEC
I decided to pick G-Money when he was a dog, and now he's the favorite, so maybe I'm tapped into the matrix on this fight, but I'm not confident. Gauge has one win in the UFC, and it was a competitive decision against Maheshate, who's not very good and who has no notable accomplishments in the promotion outside of flash KO'ing our favorite simian fighter Steve Garcia. So, I'm not sold on Gauge Young as a prospect, but he can strike and he can wrestle, and he's only 25, so he's got a lot of room left to grow. Moises, meanwhile, is 31, but he's been in the UFC forever, and it sure looks like he's slowing down and getting clunkier. He's been training with the Fighting Nerds, which might mean we see him throwing hands a bit better here. He'd already been training with the Nerds for the Gordon fight where he got slumped badly, but I'm pretty sure he'd only been with the Nerds for a few weeks at that point. Regardless, I think Young can probably outstrike Moises on the feet, which means the fight will be decided based on whether Moises can get takedowns and control time. Based on Gauge Young's fight with Salkilld, I think that's a tough ask of Moises. Although Salkilld got more than a half dozen takedowns, Young was constantly getting back to his feet and making it difficult for Salkilld to rack up much control time. And that's Quillan Salkilld. DWCS Salkilld, but still Salkilld. If this were Moises a few years ago, I'd probably pick him, but I feel better picking the up-and-comer who I see more and more good things from in every fight rather than the declining vet who I'm seeing more and more bad things from in every fight. But it's a toss-up.

Buzukja vs. Barbosa
Márcio Barbosa, TKO, R1
I've got some questions about how far Barbosa will go in the UFC because it seems like he's R1 KO or bust, but I don't think this is going to be the fight where any of his potential deficits get exposed.

Valentin vs. Leblanc
Robert Valentin, SUB, R1
Dear God. I'm pretty sure I once put Robert Valentin on a never pick list, but here we are. I watched a couple of Leblanc's fights, and he's just so slow and unathletic. He looks like me if you put me in a cage fight. He's not a DWCS guy, he's not a champion in another promotion, he's just a regional Canadian. If Leblanc wins, I'll feel like an absolute idiot, but Valentin showed actual skills on TUF. He tends to make bad decisions in the cage, but hopefully he'll craft it up here since he's definitely going to get cut if he loses.

Boser vs. Saricam
Gokhan Saricam, TKO, R1
I watched Tanner Boser's last fight and he couldn't finish Vinicius Moreira even though Moreira was gassed by the end of R1. They just sort of stopped the fight in R3. I didn't even bother watching tape on Saricam which might be a mistake, but it seems like they just threw Boser on this card because he's Canadian.

Croden vs. Zheleznyakova
Melissa Croden, DEC
So, uh, both of these ladies have kind of disappointed me, but I’ve got to go with my crodie Croden here. While Croden put up a good fight against Thainara Lisboa and found the finish, she looked really slow and ploddy in there. And while Darya beat the absolute piss out of Melissa Mullins, she still managed to get taken down and controlled despite being on the knife's edge of a R1 TKO. These ladies are both decent but have rather glaring holes in their games. While I'm not a big fan of Croden's striking, especially late in a fight, I think it's good enough to keep up with Darya, and I think Darya's ground game is pretty abysmal. Melissa's ground game isn't great (as we saw in her last fight), but she's got some good knees and elbows in the clinch and some good ground and pound when she's on top. I think that's the biggest difference-maker here.

Aldrich vs. Horth
Jamey-Lyn Horth, DEC
I did not do tape study at all on this, but I remember being shocked at Horth dog-walking Tereza Bleda, so I'm gonna go with her.

Castañeda vs. Vologdin
John Castañeda, DEC
This is another pick that I don't feel good about at all. Vologdin obviously deserves a contract after that DWCS fight, but I don't like picking DWCS alums in their debut if it's a pickem, especially when they didn't really win on DWCS in the first place. Vologdin has a lot going for him in this bout. He's almost a decade younger, he's riding in with a ton of hype, and he's definitely got the strength and physicality advantage over Castaneda. But Castaneda's not helpless on the feet. He's beaten decent competition in the UFC like Kyung Ho Kang, Muin Gafurov, and Miles Johns. I don't like the way Castaneda insists on striking when he ought to be shooting takedowns, and he might not be able to get any takedowns here since Vologdin is likely going to be putting him on the back-foot from bell to bell. This is a coin toss, but I feel like I have to go with the guy with more high-level experience.

Siraj vs. Yannis
Jamie Siraj, SUB, R2
Idk bro

posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 12 Winners, 3 Perfect, 465 Points
|
Tied for 2062nd

Burns vs. Malott
Mike Malott, DEC
I'm going to be honest, I thought Burns looked the best he's looked in awhile on the scales. I just don't think there's any durability left to give Mallot a serious test here over 25 minutes. I do think he can do well enough early to avoid getting finished and actually won't be surprised if this goes the distance. I think the grappling threat from both will cancel itself out.

Phillips vs. Jourdain
Charles Jourdain, SUB, R3
Kyler is going to shoot a bad takedown at some point once he starts to gas like he always does and hand Jourdain his third straight win by guillotine on a silver platter.

Nallo vs. Herbert
Mandel Nallo, SUB, R2
Jai can hang on the feet for sure but Nallos grappling and over all game is under-rated. Both guys are bigger lightweights but Nallo is strong as hell and likely comes out on top of any grappling exchanges.

Jasudavicius vs. Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Competitive fight but Karine's gas tank is just awful. She is a finisher who relies on her strength and athleticism but unfortunately for her JJ has plenty of both.

Moisés vs. Young
Gauge Young, DEC
Solid chance Gee Money finds a finish but I will conservatively take him here by decision. Moises lacks the wrestling to get this fight where it needs to be. I just have a feeling he's going to attempt to show off some improvements he's been working on with his striking and its just going to get him knocked out by someone whos younger, faster and hungrier.

Buzukja vs. Barbosa
Márcio Barbosa, TKO, R1
Death.

Valentin vs. Leblanc
Robert Valentin, SUB, R2
I think Valentins win over Paddy Mccory on TUF is more impressive than anything Leblanc has done so far but we're also talking about a guy that got finished by Ryan Loder. Both of these guys are pretty low level but Leblanc doesn't even look good for a regional fighter.

Boser vs. Saricam
Gokhan Saricam, DEC
I'm not gonna lie, this fight is probably going to be absolutely terrible. I hope I'm wrong but I think Saricam wins a boring decision here where he mostly just hugs Boser to death.

Croden vs. Zheleznyakova
Darya Zheleznyakova, DEC
Not a Croden fan. I think she kind of coasts on being big and athletic. Darya actually has skills and I thought she looked pretty good in here last fight against someone I think is comparable to Croden.

Aldrich vs. Horth
Jamey-Lyn Horth, DEC
Competitive but JJ is pretty much at a slight disadvantage everywhere this fight goes. Horth is just stronger, more well rounded and doesn't get hit much. I actually wouldn't be surprised if she found a second straight finish here because her hands looked really good in her last fight.

Castañeda vs. Vologdin
John Castañeda, DEC
Feel like Vologdin needs a knock-out here to be honest, which is possible. Castaneda hasn't looked very good lately but I have to take him based on his experience and how well rounded he is. I thought Vologdins DWCS fight was fun but to be honest he displayed that he's just really tough and athletic more than anything else.

Siraj vs. Yannis
Jamie Siraj, SUB, R2
Yannis doesn't seem like he can grapple to save his life and has had issues consistently making 135. Didn't look great on the scales either. Siraj is kind of a wild fighter and hasn't fought in this weight class in a really long time, but I have a feeling there will just be a strength disadvantage here that will be very noticeable once they start grappling.

Ppl who picked Herbert was based only off of him completely dominating Topuria for 99% of the fight. And basing also on overhyping Topuria, an alright wrestler who only spams hooks mindlessly like an idiot and has NEVER beat anyone in their prime (ieTsarukyan would beat him handily) like super rehydrated Volk and Max coming back down from lightweight in their mid 30's (he fought the worst version ever of Volk and Max and barely won after losing almost the whole fight spamming mindless overhands waiting for them to land). Yeah, if you read this and you get butthurt, dislike this and move on, but facts are facts: Topuria has trash distance control, no kicking ability whatsoever, bad head movement and only saved by his decent chin, no top game or top control the rare occasions he manages to use his mid wrestling ability
